reasoning: The 30-year TIPS yield (DFII30) was at 2.43% on Feb 27, 2026, which is lower than the ~2.55% levels seen earlier in February. Short-term momentum is distinctly downward, with 4 consecutive drops leading to the cutoff date. The FRED linear trend estimate combined with seasonality suggests a lower probability of increase for the first week (around 0.41-0.50). For the 1-week horizon (March 8), downward momentum likely persists, making a decrease more probable. By March 31, the forecast horizon is extended to one month. Over a monthly horizon, real yields exhibit stronger mean-reverting properties. Given that 2.43% is in the lower half of its 6-month range (from 2.38% to 2.67%), mean reversion acts an upward pull over the longer horizon. Furthermore, there is an upcoming FOMC meeting (March 17-18) where rates will be discussed, potentially adding volatility. Thus, for the March 31 date, the probability regresses strongly toward 0.5 as uncertainty increases.
